humanities_role_explanation: Designed specifically for linguistic understanding of idiomatic expressions - a core language and linguistics phenomenon - rather than using humanities data as factual test items
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Multi-modal benchmark combining text (context sentences with idioms), static images (depicting literal/figurative meanings), and temporal image sequences (3-panel comic-like representations). Also provides text-only version using image captions.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Task A requires ranking images by semantic alignment with idiomatic vs literal meanings in context. Task B involves predicting sequence completion while distinguishing literal/idiomatic senses. Both require deep semantic understanding of figurative language beyond surface-level pattern matching.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Specialized benchmark focused specifically on idiomaticity representation in multimodal contexts

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluated GPT-4, QWEN, LLaVA, BLIP, DeepSeek, RoBERTa, and other large language and vision-language models

llm_is_primary_system.note: LLMs and VLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked for idiomaticity understanding

arts_humanities_data.note: Language and Linguistics - focuses on idiomatic expressions, multi-word expressions, and figurative language understanding across English and Portuguese

quantitative_metrics.note: Top Image Accuracy, Discounted Cumulative Gain (DCG), Image completion accuracy, Sentence type accuracy

english_language.note: Paper written in English, benchmark includes both English and Portuguese data

summary: AdMIRe introduces a multimodal benchmark for evaluating LLMs' understanding of idiomatic expressions through image ranking and sequence completion tasks. The dataset contains 100 English and 55 Portuguese idiomatic compounds with corresponding visual representations of their literal and figurative meanings.
key_findings: Top-performing systems achieved human-level performance (93% accuracy) but required substantial computational resources, mixture-of-experts approaches, and careful prompt engineering. LLMs showed bias toward idiomatic interpretations regardless of context, and multimodal approaches generally outperformed text-only methods.
